# '--' is mandatory before the command. The two forms below both
# work and produce equivalent jobs:
#
#   eon add ... -- "echo hello"          → /bin/sh -c "echo hello"
#   eon add ... -- /bin/echo hello       → exec direct
#   eon add ... -- sh -c "echo hello"    → exec direct (explicit shell)

# Without '--' → hard fail.
! exec eon add --at '+1h' --name broken1 'echo hello'
stderr 'Usage error.*place the command'
! exec eon add --at '+1h' --name broken2 /bin/echo hi
stderr 'Usage error.*place the command'
! exec eon add --at '+1h' --name broken3 /bin/echo
stderr 'Usage error.*place the command'

# Single quoted string after '--' → wrapped in sh -c.
exec eon add --at '+1h' --name shellform -- 'echo hello'
stdout 'added job'
exec eon show shellform --json
stdout '/bin/sh'
stdout '-c'
stdout 'echo hello'

# Multi-arg after '--' → direct argv.
exec eon add --at '+1h' --name directform -- /bin/echo hi
stdout 'added job'
exec eon show directform --json
stdout '/bin/echo'

# Explicit sh -c after '--' → direct argv (user picks the shell).
exec eon add --at '+1h' --name explicit -- sh -c 'echo new'
exec eon show explicit --json
stdout '"sh"'
stdout '"-c"'
stdout '"echo new"'

# Absolute-path binary that doesn't exist is rejected at add time, so
# users find out before the daemon ever fires the job. Catches the
# common cross-distro slip where someone types /bin/true on macOS (it
# lives at /usr/bin/true) or similar.
! exec eon add --at '+1h' --name nonexistent -- /no/such/binary
stderr 'command not found: /no/such/binary'

# Same rule for the shell-form when the line is a bare absolute path.
! exec eon add --at '+1h' --name nonexistent2 -- /no/such/binary
stderr 'command not found'

# But a shell line with metacharacters is opaque to us; we let it
# through and the failure surfaces in the run log if the user typo'd.
exec eon add --at '+1h' --name shellopaque -- '/no/such/binary || true'
stdout 'added job'

# Relative paths and bare names are not validated (PATH/CWD differ
# between CLI and daemon at fire time).
exec eon add --at '+1h' --name relpath -- ./nope.sh arg
stdout 'added job'
exec eon add --at '+1h' --name barename -- nope-not-on-path
stdout 'added job'
